---
title: "Count Data"
subtitle: "Regression for counts and rates"
---

## Overview

Counts are everywhere in health economics: the number of GP visits a patient makes in a year, hospital readmissions, falls, prescriptions filled. These outcomes are non-negative integers, often skewed, frequently piled up at zero --- and OLS handles none of that gracefully.

This module develops the count-data toolkit. Poisson regression is the natural starting point, with its log link and clean interpretation of coefficients as rate ratios. But the Poisson model makes a strong and usually false promise --- that the variance equals the mean --- so we confront overdispersion and the Negative Binomial fix, and then the very common problem of excess zeros, handled by zero-inflated and hurdle models. Throughout, the offset lets us model rates per person-year or per thousand listed patients.

Like the discrete-choice models, these are maximum-likelihood estimators built on the [Maximum Likelihood & GMM](../11-mle-gmm/index.qmd) foundation.

## Pages in This Module

- [Poisson Regression](poisson-regression.qmd) --- Modeling counts and rates.
- [Overdispersion and the Negative Binomial](overdispersion-negbin.qmd) --- When the variance exceeds the mean.
- [Excess Zeros: Hurdle and Zero-Inflated Models](zero-inflation.qmd) --- Two processes, one count.
